answer: Telegram API authentication for user accounts is based on an encryption key identifier (auth_key_id) [1]. The standard login flow begins by invoking auth.sendCode, which returns a phone_code_hash used to verify the user via auth.signIn [1][2]. If two-factor authentication is enabled, the server returns a SESSION_PASSWORD_NEEDED error, requiring the client to verify the password using the Secure Remote Password (SRP) protocol via auth.checkPassword [1][3][4]. Alternative login methods include QR code and passkey flows [1]. For the separate Gateway API, authentication is managed via an access token passed in the header or as a parameter [5].
